summary: Drew DeVault's February 2020 status update covers progress across his free software projects following FOSDEM. Sway 1.4 and wlroots 0.10.0 were released along with related Wayland tools. The email client aerc gained contextual configuration options, while SourceHut achieved full WCAG accessibility compliance and added file attachments for git tags. He teases a new secret project he's keeping under wraps until he knows it will pan out, which would later turn out to be the Hare programming language.
key_points: Sway 1.4 and wlroots 0.10.0 were finally released alongside updates to swayidle, swaylock, and swaybg
SourceHut achieved 100% compliance with WCAG accessibility guidelines and scores perfectly on performance and web standards tests
aerc gained contextual configuration options allowing different settings based on regex matching of email properties
A secret new project is consuming significant development time, kept under wraps until viability is proven
money_quotes: Putting faces to names and getting to know everyone on a personal level is really important to me, and I would love FOSDEM even if that was all I got out of it.
SourceHut is now fully complaint with the WCAG accessibility guidelines. We now score 100% on standard performance, accessibility, and web standards compliance tests.
SourceHut is the lightest weight, most usable forge.
I recently fixed a bug report from a Lynx user, too.
I intend to keep this project under wraps for a while still, because I don't want people to start using it before I know if it's going to pan out or not.
I hope you'll bear with me for a while and trust that the results will speak for themselves.
key_insight: DeVault's monthly update showcases steady, community-driven progress across the Wayland, email, and SourceHut ecosystems while hinting at an ambitious secret project that would demand patience from supporters.
